description: Amazon selected Long Island City, Queens for HQ2 in Nov 2018 with $3B in subsidies. Initial public support ~56% eroded to ~45% as progressive backlash over corporate welfare intensified. Amazon withdrew Feb 2019. Tests elite-grassroots conflict and subsidy backlash dynamics.

key_events.description: Amazon announces Long Island City as HQ2 site with $3B in state/city tax incentives. Quinnipiac poll shows 56% NYC support.
NYC Council hearing turns contentious. AOC and local officials publicly oppose deal. Progressive opposition coalesces.
Siena College poll shows support dropping to 48% among NYC residents as subsidy details become clearer.
Amazon executives refuse to commit to union neutrality at council hearing. Anti-Amazon coalition intensifies protests.
Amazon withdraws HQ2 from NYC on Feb 14, 2019, citing political hostility. Post-withdrawal polls show divided opinion.

calibration_notes: Tests corporate-populist backlash dynamics. Key patterns: initial majority support eroded by organized progressive opposition, subsidy framing shifted from 'jobs' to 'corporate welfare', elite support (Cuomo, de Blasio) insufficient against grassroots mobilization, geographic polarization (Queens residents more supportive than broader NYC).
